steps.title: Simplify the given equation
steps.content: Start with ${11}(x-n)=11y+11n$. Expanding gives $11x-11n=11y+11n$, so $11x-11y=22n$. Divide by $11$ to get $$x-y=2n.$$

steps.title: Know the condition for no solution
steps.content: A two-line system has no solution when the lines are parallel and distinct. So the second equation should have the same left side pattern as $x-y=2n$ but a different constant.

steps.title: Compare the choices
steps.content:
The correct answer is
$$3x-3y=3n$$
Divide by $3$:
$$x-y=n$$
This is parallel to $x-y=2n$ but not the same line because $n$ is positive.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: $3x - 3y = 6n$ simplifies to the same line as the original, so it gives infinitely many solutions. The equations with $x+y$ have slope $-1$ after simplification, so they cross the original. $3x - 3y = 3n$ is parallel but separate, so there is no solution.
